name: missing-tcpa-consent
description: Lead-capture form collects a phone number for SMS outreach but is missing the express written consent disclosure TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act) requires. Compliance agent must flag and locate it.

page.purpose: Lead-capture: phone-number form for SMS follow-up callbacks.
page.userFunction: Visitor submits their phone number to receive a callback or text from the company.

matchedRules.description: Forms collecting a phone number for marketing SMS or autodialed calls MUST display an express written consent disclosure adjacent to the submit control, naming the company and stating that consent is not a condition of purchase.

findings.problem: The phone-collecting form has no TCPA express written consent disclosure adjacent to the submit button. The form posts to /api/leads which is positioned for marketing SMS/voice follow-up.
findings.plain: Your contact form asks for a phone number but doesn't include the legally required note that says 'by submitting, you consent to be contacted by company name'. The TCPA — a US law that governs marketing calls and texts — requires this on every form like this one.
findings.whyItMatters: Submitting marketing texts or autodialed calls to a number captured without this disclosure exposes the business to TCPA suits with statutory damages of $500–$1,500 per call or text. The risk scales with every lead the form collects.
findings.requiredFix: Add a checkbox or visible disclosure adjacent to the submit button along the lines of: 'By clicking Submit, you agree that <Company Name> may contact you at the phone number provided, including by SMS and autodialed calls, for marketing purposes. Consent is not a condition of any purchase. Message and data rates may apply.'
